mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid one-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears and equity sell-offs within the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or trigger activation shows no statistically significant drop in subsequent weekly returns.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in revolving consumer credit and housing starts indicates robust household liquidity and construction activity, which jointly boost aggregate demand and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal shows zero predictive power for 60-day forward returns, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production sustaining levels above 103 demonstrates supply chain resilience and capacity utilization, supporting corporate profit margins and equity multiples over a one-month forward window.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not significantly exceed the baseline median when INDPRO crosses the 103 threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly expands the corporate earnings base, creating a mechanical upward drift in equity valuations that persists into the following week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show a correlation coefficient below 0.05 with 5-day forward returns across all available quarterly observations, or the signal fails to predict returns in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent consumer price inflation exceeding an index level of 315 forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, elevating the equity risk premium and depressing valuation multiples over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: High CPI periods do not produce negative 60-day forward returns, or the signal's information coefficient remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window despite persistent inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously increasing unemployment rate indicates structural labor market friction and wage-push inflation, which compresses equity multiples over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, or the joint distribution of PAYEMS and UNRATE changes shows no statistically significant relationship with subsequent monthly equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated revolving credit balances exceeding 720000 million dollars signal strong consumer liquidity and spending capacity, providing immediate upward pressure on equity prices during periods of credit expansion.
failure_condition: High RSAFS levels fail to predict positive 5-day returns, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% in a walk-forward evaluation over a 2-year period.
